signals.title: USD/CAD — Geopolitical Shock + FOMC Minutes
signals.text: WTI Crude plunges -4.87% to $99.1 on US-Iran diplomatic optimism and potential Strait of Hormuz reopening, stripping CAD of its energy-linked support. Simultaneously, FOMC minutes reinforce a 'higher-for-longer' stance with some members favoring the removal of easing bias. Fed 3.75% vs BoC 2.25% provides a +150bp carry advantage for USD, which is now supported by both a hawkish policy floor and deteriorating terms of trade for CAD. USD/CAD at 1.3740.

signals.title: GBP/AUD — G8 Bilateral Theme + Carry Divergence
signals.text: GBP/AUD spread reaches 1.199% as GBP outperforms the G8 complex while AUD remains the weekly laggard. Despite a -60bp carry deficit (BoE 3.75% vs RBA 4.35%), GBP strength is driven by risk-on equity flows (S&P 500 +1.08%) and falling US yields, which disproportionately benefit GBP over the commodity-linked AUD. AUD/USD COT LF net long +55,794 (74.8%) suggests a crowded long position vulnerable to further liquidation if commodity prices remain under pressure. GBP/USD at 1.3441, AUD/USD at 0.7156.
signals.watch level: RBNZ meeting on 27 May for regional sentiment spillover

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence + RBNZ
signals.text: NZD/USD COT LF net short -19,437 (22.3% long) shows speculators heavily fading the kiwi despite a hawkish RBNZ market bias (Hike). With the RBNZ meeting on 27 May, the crowded spec short position faces significant squeeze risk if the central bank delivers a hawkish surprise or formalizes the hike bias. NZD/USD at 0.5873 remains supported by the broader USD pullback following the oil price collapse.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Volatility Premium + Positioning
signals.text: AUD/USD IV at 25.3% vs HV30 at 8.9% generates a massive +16.4% Vol Risk Premium (VRP), signaling extreme institutional demand for downside protection despite the 1D price bounce. 25d RR at -0.97 confirms a moderate put skew as the options market hedges against a reversal in the crowded COT LF net long (+55,794). The divergence between spot recovery and extreme IV suggests market participants are paying up for tail-risk protection. AUD/USD at 0.7156.

signals.title: USD/JPY — Carry vs Options Skew
signals.text: USD/JPY at 158.85 remains anchored by the +280bp SOFR-TONA carry advantage (Fed 3.75% vs BoJ 0.75%). However, the options market is pricing in intervention risk with a 25d RR of -1.44 (moderate put skew), diverging from the crowded JPY COT LF net short of -70,605. This indicates that while speculators are chasing carry, the options market is aggressively hedging for a sharp JPY recovery. Carry remains the dominant driver while VIX stays at 17.4 (normal volatility).
signals.watch level: 160.00 psychological level for BoJ intervention risk
